mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which mechanically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points over two months triggers a repricing of equity risk premiums as discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm a broad demand expansion that is more powerful than either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 60% of instances when both INDPRO and RSAFS rise simultaneously, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate sits below 5%, further month-over-month declines signal labor market tightness and rising wage costs that compress corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE is below 5%, or the signal underperforms its unconditional baseline by less than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals a negative supply shock that reduces corporate earnings visibility, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in consumer prices and fall in payroll growth signals stagflationary pressure where the Federal Reserve is constrained from easing despite slowing growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises MoM and PAYEMS falls MoM
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during months where CPIAUCSL rises and PAYEMS falls (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in housing starts exceeding 5% signals weakening construction activity and durable goods demand, which precedes a broader demand slowdown and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when HOUST falls more than 5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month rise in industrial production and fall in unemployment confirms a positive supply-demand loop where output expands without inflationary bottlenecks, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises MoM and UNRATE falls MoM
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.
